P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 ••• 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 958U 
Anthony S. Alperin 
Deputy City Attorney 
City of Los Angeles 
City Hall 
Los Angeles, CA 
90012 
Dear Mr. Alperin: 
August 16, 1978 
Re: 
Opinion Request 
No. 78-010 
.Thank you for your letter of Jcrl~_18, 1978, requesting an 
opinion regarding the conflict of interest provisions of 
the Political Reform Act. 
Because your question does not 
raise a substantial issue of interpretation under the 
Political Reform Act, the Commission has denied your re-
quest for a formal opinion. 
I hope the following informal 
advic~ is helpful. 
In your le~ter you have asked whether the South Coast Air 
Quality Management District (South Coast) is considered 
a state agency for the purposes of the Political Reform 
Act, and whether lobbyist activity with regard to the 
District need be reported. 
We have provided advice "on 
several previous occasions that South Coast is not a 
state agency. 
Government Code Section 82049 defines state 
agency to include: 
... every state office, department, division, 
bureau, board and commission, and the Legis-
lature, but does not include the courts or any 
agency in the judicial branch of government. 
The Commission has provided by regulation that: 
An agency is a state agency within the pro-
·visions of Government Code Section 82049 
only if all the following criteria are met: 


-1 
1 
I 
j 
i 
1 
i ! 
.Anthony S. Alperin 
August 16 , 1978 
Page 2 
Ca) 
The agency is authorized by statute, 
executive order or the California 
constitution. 
(b) 
At least one voting member is an 
elected state officer or is appointed 
by an elected state officer or an 
agency official or a state agency. 
ec) 
The agency is financed in part by any 
state funds or is subject to appro-
priation in the state budget. 
(d) 
An area larger than one county is 
included in its jurisdiction. 
2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18249 
Although South Coast satisfies'~~~ criteria set forth in 
2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18249, there are certain overriding 
factors which lead to the conclusion that South Coast is 
local in nature al1d not a state agency. 
First., most air 
quality or air pollution districts are limited in their juris-
diction to one county. 
Second, over 90% of the 
finances 
for such districts comes from local funds. 
Third, the 
California,Air Resources Board (CARE) is the true state 
agency in the air pollution area. 
The CARE adopts regula-
tions which supersede all local regulations and bind' all 
local districts including South Coast. 
Moreover, all 
variances and permits granted by a local district must be 
approved by the CARB. 
It is our conclusion that South Coast is not a state agency. 
Therefore, attempts to influence rule-making decisions of 
the District need not be reported pursuant to Chapter 6 of 
the Act. 
I hope this information has been helpful. 
If you have any 
questions regarding this matter, please contact me or Dwight 
Dickerson, an attorney in our Conflicts of Interest Division. 
If you wish to appeal the denial of your request tor a formal 
opinion, you may do so pursuant to 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 
18321. 
• 
Sincerely, 
Michael Bennett 
Executive Director 


'. 
OFFICI! OF 
CITY ATTORNEY 
CITY HALL 
t: 
L.OS ANGEL.ES. CAL.IFORNIA 90012 
BURT PINES 
CITY ATTORNEY 
July 18, 1978 
The Honorable Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
1100 "K" Street Building 
Sacramento, California 
95814 
Honorable Members: 
JUL II 
Req~ No78 010 
Date Received ? -;;; } - ? 
/-7'-: 7J 
~se Due ______ _ 
" 1.1 t\" ,.., 
From time to time ~"attorneys of this office and" 
members of the staffs of. departments and offices of the 
City of Los Angeles, including Deputy City Attorney Gregory 
O'Brien, attempt-to influence rule-making'decisions of the 
South Coast Air Quality Management District. 
If the 
District is a "state agency" within the meaning of the 
Political Reform Act, then the City is required to report 
the above activities on its monthly statements filed 
pursuant to Chapter 6 of the Act, and persons, including 
Mr. OIBrien, may qualify as lobbyists in the future. 
Although the District appears to meet the test for 
a "state agency" set forth at 2 Cal. Admin. Code Section 18249, 
your staff has orally advised us that the District should not 
be treated as such. 
The formal opinion of the Commission is requested 
concerning whether the District is a "state agency" and 
concerning the consequent duties of the City and its employees 
who attempt to influence actions of the District. 
In the 
event that the Commission does not issue an opinion, written 
advice on the subject is requested, pursuant to Government 
Code Section 83114(b). 
AS}\: gj h 
485-5418 
cc: 
Gregory OtBrien, Esq. 
Very truly yours, 
BURT PINES, City Attorney 
By ~5!u~~ 
Deputy City Attorney

sections.conclusion: It is our conclusion that South Coast is not a state agency. Therefore, attempts to influence rule-making decisions of the District need not be reported pursuant to Chapter 6 of the Act.
sections.question_synthetic: Is the South Coast Air Quality Management District a state agency under the Political Reform Act, and must attempts to influence its rule-making decisions be reported as lobbyist activity?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC determined that South Coast is not a state agency despite meeting certain regulatory criteria, because it is primarily local in nature, funded mostly from local sources (over 90%), and subordinate to the California Air Resources Board. Therefore, lobbyist reporting is not required for attempts to influence South Coast's rule-making decisions.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: ocr; LLM: Document includes both the incoming request (City Attorney's letter dated July 18, 1978) and the FPPC response (Deputy Executive Director Bennett's letter dated August 16, 1978). The FPPC denied the request for a formal opinion but provided informal advice. Clear question and conclusion sections present.

embedding.summary: The FPPC provided informal advice declining a formal opinion request regarding whether the South Coast Air Quality Management District is a state agency under the Political Reform Act. The FPPC concluded South Coast is not a state agency and therefore attempts to influence its decisions need not be reported as lobbyist activity.
